2605.06318.title: Who and What? Using Linguistic Features and Annotator Characteristics to Analyze Annotation Variation
2605.06318.paper_summary: Human label variation has been established as a central phenomenon in NLP: the perspectives different annotators have on the same item need to be embraced. Data collection practices thus shifted towards increasing the annotator numbers and releasing disaggregated datasets, harmful language being most resourced due to its high subjectivity. While this resulted in rich information about \textit{who} annotated (sociodemographics, attitudes, etc.), the \textit{what} (e.g., linguistic properties of items), and their interplay has received little attention. We present the first large-scale analysis of four reference datasets for harmful language detection, bringing together annotator characteristics, linguistic properties of the items, and their interactions in a statistically informed picture. We find that interactions are crucial, revealing intersectional effects ignored in previous work, and that a strong role is played by lexical cues and annotator attitudes. Effect patterns, however, vary considerably across datasets. This urges caution about generalization and transferability.
2605.06318.paper_summary_zh: 人類標註變異已被確立為自然語言處理中的一個核心現象：不同標註者對同一項目的看法需要被接受。<lb/>因此，數據收集實踐轉向增加標註者人數並釋放分解數據集，因其高度主觀性，對有害語言的資源投入最多。<lb/>雖然這導致了關於\textit{誰}進行標註（社會人口統計、態度等）的豐富信息，但\textit{什麼}（例如，項目的語言特性）及其相互作用卻鮮有關注。<lb/>我們呈現了四個有害語言檢測參考數據集的首次大規模分析，將標註者特徵、項目的語言特性及其相互作用整合成一幅統計上知情的圖景。<lb/>我們發現相互作用至關重要，揭示了以往研究中被忽視的交叉效應，並且詞彙線索和標註者態度扮演了重要角色。<lb/>然而，效應模式在不同數據集之間變化顯著。<lb/>這提醒我們在推廣和轉移性方面需謹慎。
2605.06318.author: Maximilian Maurer et.al.
2605.06318.authors: Maximilian Maurer, Maximilian Linde, Gabriella Lapesa
